instructions.instruction: Log all four correlation fields (channel, message_id, user_id, timetoken) on every send and every receive
instructions.original_snippets: Every send and receive code path logs **at minimum**: | `channel` | The PubNub channel name | | `message_id` | The client-generated UUID for idempotent publish | | `user_id` | The PubNub `userId` of the publisher (and the subscriber, separately) | | `timetoken` | The server-assigned 17-digit timetoken |
instructions.relevant_when: Implementing any PubNub publish or receive code path

instructions.instruction: Always emit structured logs (JSON or key=value), never free-form console.log strings
instructions.original_snippets: Always emit structured logs (JSON or key=value), never `console.log("Sent " + channel)`. ... Free-form string logs | Structured key=value or JSON
instructions.relevant_when: Adding logging anywhere in a PubNub code path

instructions.instruction: Log connection-state events on the status listener with category, operation, affectedChannels, lastTimetoken, currentTimetoken
instructions.original_snippets: pubnub.addListener({ status: (s) => { log.info('pubnub.status', { category: s.category, operation: s.operation, affectedChannels: s.affectedChannels, lastTimetoken: s.lastTimetoken, currentTimetoken: s.currentTimetoken }); } });
instructions.relevant_when: Setting up listeners on a PubNub instance

instructions.instruction: Sample high-volume logs by message_id hash (deterministic), not random, so all logs for a sampled message are kept together
instructions.original_snippets: Sample by **`message_id` hash**, not random — so you keep all logs for a sampled message ... Random sampling that drops correlated events | Sample by `message_id` hash
instructions.relevant_when: Adding log sampling on a high-volume publish or receive path

instructions.instruction: Always log errors and connection-state changes in full — never sample those
instructions.original_snippets: Always log **errors** and **status changes** in full (no sampling).
instructions.relevant_when: Configuring sampling rules

instructions.instruction: PubNub bills by transactions, not bytes — fan-out × publish rate is the dominant cost driver
instructions.original_snippets: PubNub bills by **transactions**, not bytes. The dominant cost drivers in most apps: ... The single largest factor in most apps is **fan-out × publish rate**.
instructions.relevant_when: Estimating, projecting, or investigating PubNub cost

instructions.instruction: Project cost before launch using the 5-step audit: publish rate, fan-out, history rate, function invocations, app context operations — multiplied by projected DAU
instructions.original_snippets: Before any new feature ships, walk through: 1. **Publish rate**: how many publishes per second per active user? 2. **Fan-out**: per channel, how many subscribers? 3. **History rate**: how often are history reads triggered? 4. **Function invocations**: how many per publish? 5. **App Context operations**: how often per session? Multiply by your projected DAU.
instructions.relevant_when: Auditing cost for a new PubNub feature before launch

instructions.instruction: Coalesce updates on the producer side when state changes faster than the UI renders (cursor position, scroll, game tick deltas, sensor 'latest wins')
instructions.original_snippets: When state changes faster than a UI can render, **coalesce on the producer side**: ... For a cursor that moves 60 times per second, this drops publish rate to 10/s with no visible UX change. ... Cursor / pointer position | Yes (10–30 Hz max) ... Game tick (deltas) | Yes (30 Hz typical) ... Sensor reading where only latest matters | Yes
instructions.relevant_when: Publishing rapidly-changing state where only the latest value matters to subscribers

instructions.instruction: Do NOT coalesce chat messages, order placement events, or other discrete events where every message matters
instructions.original_snippets: Chat message | No — every message matters | Order placement | No — every event is discrete
instructions.relevant_when: Deciding whether to coalesce a particular update stream

instructions.instruction: Use signal() for ephemeral updates (typing indicators, presence-like updates) instead of publish() — cheaper and ~64-byte cap
instructions.original_snippets: `signal()` | No | Yes | Ephemeral signals (typing indicators, presence-like updates); cheaper than `pubnub.publish()`, smaller max payload (~64 bytes) | ... `publish()` for ephemeral signals | `signal()` (also smaller payload limit)
instructions.relevant_when: Sending an ephemeral indicator that doesn't need to be received-and-stored

instructions.instruction: Use fire() to trigger server-side Functions without delivering to subscribers
instructions.original_snippets: `fire()` | No | Yes | Triggering server-side Functions only; not delivered to subscribers | ... `publish()` for fire-only Function triggers | `fire()`
instructions.relevant_when: Triggering Functions without subscriber delivery

instructions.instruction: Test pyramid: unit (envelope/reducer/dedup logic), integration (round-trip on test keyset), load (fan-out concurrency), E2E (real devices in staging)
instructions.original_snippets: | Unit | Envelope shape, schema versioning, reducer logic | | Integration | Full publish → subscribe round trip in a test keyset | | Load | Fan-out, presence updates, history fetch concurrency | | End-to-end | Real device flows in staging |
instructions.relevant_when: Designing a test plan for a PubNub feature

instructions.instruction: Use a dedicated test keyset for integration tests — never test against prod, and never use demo keys
instructions.original_snippets: Use a dedicated test keyset (separate from dev — see [environment separation]) ... Integration tests against prod keyset | Use a dedicated test keyset ... Demo keyset behavior | Demo keys are public and throttled; they're not your prod keys
instructions.relevant_when: Setting up an integration test environment

instructions.instruction: Use unique channel names per test (test-${uuid()}) so parallel test runs do not collide
instructions.original_snippets: Use unique channel names per test (`test-${uuid()}`; see [naming]) so parallel test runs don't collide. ... Shared channel name across parallel test runs | Use a unique channel per test (`test-${uuid()}`)
instructions.relevant_when: Writing integration tests with shared infrastructure

instructions.instruction: Pull get_pubnub_usage_metrics per-keyset (per-environment) — mixing environments distorts the picture
instructions.original_snippets: Pull per-environment (per-keyset) — see [keysets-and-environments]. Mixing environments distorts the picture. ... One big shared keyset across envs makes attribution impossible | One keyset per env; metrics are per-keyset
instructions.relevant_when: Pulling usage metrics for cost reconciliation or capacity planning

instructions.instruction: Cost spike investigation starts with per-category breakdown — never just total transactions
instructions.original_snippets: 1. **Pull usage metrics for the spike window. Categorize by transaction type (publish, subscribe, function, history, app context, presence).** 2. **Identify the dominant transaction type.** Most cost spikes come from one category. ... Cost spike investigated without categorizing transactions | Always start with per-category breakdown
instructions.relevant_when: Investigating a cost spike

instructions.instruction: General incident triage order: (1) collect four correlation fields, (2) check status.pubnub.com, (3) pull usage metrics for window, (4) run the specific runbook
instructions.original_snippets: For **any** PubNub incident, run this sequence first: 1. **Identify the four correlation fields** ... 2. **Check PubNub status**: open `https://status.pubnub.com` ... 3. **Check `get_pubnub_usage_metrics`** for the affected keyset and time window ... 4. **Run the runbook** for the specific incident class.
instructions.relevant_when: Beginning triage on any PubNub-related production incident

instructions.instruction: Files do not belong in publish() — use the File Sharing API; base64 in publish() is the most common cost mistake
instructions.original_snippets: For files (images, PDFs, audio), use the [File Sharing API], which uploads to PubNub-managed storage and delivers a reference. Stuffing base64 in `publish()` payloads is the most common cost mistake. ... Files in `publish()` payloads | File Sharing API
instructions.relevant_when: Sending image, PDF, audio, or other file content through PubNub

instructions.instruction: Echoing publishes back to the publisher doubles fan-out — filter on receive when publisher_id === self
instructions.original_snippets: | Echoing publishes back to the publisher | Doubles fan-out | Filter on receive (publisher_id !== self) | ... Echoing publishes back to the publisher | Filter on receive (publisher_id !== self)
instructions.relevant_when: Designing a publish/subscribe pattern where the publisher also subscribes

instructions.instruction: Never log payload contents of chat messages; log shape only or hash sensitive fields (PII risk)
instructions.original_snippets: Logging payload contents in chat | PII risk; log shape only or hash sensitive fields
instructions.relevant_when: Logging messages that may contain user-generated chat content
